completedSteps.summary: Created the admin-dashboard folder at the project root.
Verified adminAuth middleware already protects admin routes by reading x-admin-key, comparing it to ADMIN_API_KEY, calling next() on success, and returning 401 JSON on failure.
Verified admin controller functions and updated getUrlStats to return the full URL document.
Updated admin routes to apply adminAuth once with router.use(adminAuth), then mapped all required admin endpoints.
Verified CORS is enabled, admin routes are mounted before public routes, and the backend does not serve admin-dashboard through Express.
Created a standalone dark admin dashboard with key-based unlock flow, global stats, URL table, edit/delete actions, stats modal, loading state, error banner, Refresh, and Logout.

verification: Read backend-session-summary.json and public-page-session-summary.json before each phase as requested by admin-dashboard-steps.json.
Verified backend/middleware/adminAuth.js met Phase 4 requirements without changes.
Ran node --check controllers/adminController.js after updating getUrlStats.
Ran node --check routes/admin.js after updating admin route middleware usage.
Ran node --check server.js and searched backend JavaScript for express.static, admin-dashboard, and public-page references for Phase 7.
Searched admin-dashboard/index.html for API_BASE, x-admin-key, localStorage adminKey, required admin endpoints, delete confirmation, PATCH, DELETE, loading spinner, Refresh, and Logout.

remainingSteps.summary: Admin dashboard related security checklist still needs to be reviewed as the next phase.
Manual admin dashboard API smoke tests still need to be run with the backend and MongoDB available.
